description: Synthetic, hand-labeled telemetry event set for benchmarking BCA (Blackwell Correlation Algorithm). Ground-truth incident boundaries were assigned by construction, not inferred -- this fixture is built so we already know the correct answer, which is the only way precision/recall on incident-boundary detection means anything. Three scenarios are included: (1) a clean two-actor case that should produce exactly 2 incidents, (2) a noisy single-actor case with an out-of-window straggler event that should split into 2 incidents despite sharing an actor, (3) an unmatched-pattern case to exercise the 0.40-confidence branch.

events.note: Deliberately placed 29m20s after syn-007 -- outside the default 300s window, so a correct implementation must split this into a SEPARATE incident from C despite the shared actor.

T1071 -> T1041 (C2 -> Exfiltration) is not in BCA's bundled KNOWN_CHAIN_PATTERNS, which only encodes this project's own VECTOR-I/II chain. A correct run should group these into one incident at the 0.40 'multiple techniques, unrecognized chain' confidence level -- this exercises that branch honestly rather than only testing the cases BCA is tuned for.
